element_introduction_order.mastery_context: One of three bomb types available early
Alternative bomb with different blast pattern
Third bomb type; most specialized weapon

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Arcade-paced scrolling shooter with constant forward momentum; tension derives from enemy density and projectile avoidance rather than pacing variation

difficulty_curve.early_game: Accessible introduction to isometric controls and weapon systems
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Escalating enemy density and projectile complexity
difficulty_curve.late_game: Described as 'unbelievably hard' in later ports; high difficulty maintained through stage progression
notable_spikes: PlayStation port conversion noted as excessively difficult across all stages
Later stages demand sustained weapon mastery and dodging precision

notable_level_patterns.description: Game uses isometric perspective (inspired by Zaxxon) where stages scroll forward; player navigates both horizontal and vertical space within the tilted 3D view
Stages composed of patterned enemy encounters that escalate in complexity; designed for arcade repetition and skill demonstration
Stages feature similar design structures; reviewers noted that level designs become repetitive, causing gameplay to feel monotonous mid-playthrough
